english: mind to perfect rest, for meditation, frq.; mnyám-pa sbyór-ba (mnyam pa sbyor ba) id. (?) - mnyám-du (mnyam du) adv. (col. *nyám-po* (*nyam po*)) c. dań (dang) : together with, in company of, blá-ma dań nyám-du _{o}grogs dús-su (bla ma dang nyam du 'grogs dus su) Mil.; ma dań mnyám-du či-ba (ma dang mnyam du ci ba) Thgy.; col. *ńa dań nyám-po šog* (*nga dang nyam po shog*) or merely *nyám-po šog* (*nyam po shog*) come along with me! *nyam sôń-te* (*nyam song te*) going along with; nyí-ma šàr-ba dań mnyám-du (nyi ma shar ba dang mnyam du) with the rising sun Mil.; col. *dul dań nyám-po* (*dul dang nyam po*) in walking, ambulando; *ten dań nyám-po* (*ten dang nyam po*) in taking it away (it was broken); *kúr-pa nyám* (*kur pa nyam*) (to send something) by (with) a cooly. - mnyam-méd (mnyam med), mnyam - brál (mnyam bral) unequalled, matchless; mi mnyám-pa (mi mnyam pa) 1. unequal, 2. uneven. - p̌yag (phyag) (or lag (lag)) ynyis (gnyis) mnyám-bźag-tu (mnyam bzhag tu) yód-pa (yod pa) both hands laid together on the stomach, mnyám-bźag p̌yág-rgya-čan (mnyam bzhag phyag rgya can) id. - mnyam-pa-nyid (mnyam pa nyid), equality, parity; impartiality, justice.

pf. and imp. mnyes (mnyes), fut. mnye (mnye), W. col. *mnyo-če* (*mnyo che*), 1. to rub, between the hands or feet, e.g. ears of corn; one's body Tar.; esp. hides, hence to tan, curry, dress; kó-ba mnyés-pa (ko ba mnyes pa) a tanned hide, dressed leather; *šed dań nyé-če* (*shed dang nye che*) W. to rub in or into with force. - 2. Cs. also: to coax.

W. *nyén-mo* (*nyen mo*), flexible, pliable, supple; soft, smooth, of the voice frq.; of the mind Dzl.; mnyén-par byéd-pa (mnyen par byed pa) to make soft, smooth, flexible, gyúr-ba (gyur ba) to become soft, of the skin etc. Med.; mnyen-mnyél-ba (mnyen mnyel ba) to make soft by tanning Sch. - mnyen-mnyés yšin-pa (mnyen mnyes gshin pa) to caress, to fondle Sch.
1. also ynyél-ba (gnyel ba), to tan, to dress (hides) Sch. - 2. resp. for ńál-ba (ngal ba) to get tired Pth.

resp. for dgá-ba (dga' ba), in more recent writings and col. for the dgyés-pa (dgyes pa) of ancient literature, to be glad, to take delight in, ccd.; to be willing, to wish, often with tugs (thugs); mnyés-par byéd-pa (mnyes par byed pa) to make glad, to give pleasure; e.g. to the king by presents Glr., to Buddha by worshipping him Glr. - mnyes-bźin-pa (mnyes bzhin pa) Lexx., Sch.: to love much; to be rejoiced at.
W. fondling, petting, p̌rú-gu-la (phru gu la) a child.
several wild-growing species of Polygonum Med.
Cs. = bšál-ba (bshal ba), to rinse; W. to suffer diarrhoea, rnyań-nád (rnyang nad) diarrhoea; rnyáń-pa (rnyang pa) diarrhetic stool; rnyáń-ma (rnyang ma), rnyáń-ma (rnyang ma) id., ni f.
to seize or snatch together Sch.

W. *nyíu* (*nyiu*), *nyiú-ńu* (*nyiu ngu*) (cf. rnyoń (rnyong)) 1. snare, for catching wild animals, rnyí _{o}dzúg-pa (rnyi 'dzug pa) to lay snares, also fig. - 2. trap, p̌úr-rnyi (phur rnyi) mouse-trap (consisting of a flat stone supported by a little stick (p̌úr-pa (phur pa)). - 3. net Sch. (?).

old, ancient, of things, e.g. clothes, gsar-rnyíń (gsar rnying) new and old snár-gyi yi-ge rnyíń-pa-rnams (snar gyi yi ge rnying pa rnams) ancient records Glr.; brda-rnyíń (brda rnying) the ancient orthography Zam.; lo-rnyíń (lo rnying) = na-níń (na ning) last year Wts.; drań-sróń rnyíń-pa (drang srong rnying pa) the old rishi, i.e. the well-known, of long standing, opp. to a new - comer Dzl. - rnyíń - ba (rnying ba) vb., pf. brnyíńs (brnyings), to grow old, gos brnyíńs (gos brnyings) old clothes, lham brnyíńs (lham brnyings) old shoes Lex.; rnyíń - bar gyúr-ba (rnying bar gyur ba) id.; rnyíń-bar byéd-pa (rnying bar byed pa) to wear out or away in a short time Dzl.
n. of the most ancient sect of Lamas, clothed in red, v. Köpp.; Schl. 72; rnyíń-ma-pa (rnying ma pa) one belonging to this sect.
pf. brnyíd (brnyid), (b)rnyis ((b)rnyis), fut. gnyid (gnyid), 1. to wither, to fade, also fig. - 2. to grieve, (vb. n.) Sch.
I. vb., pf. brnyed (brnyed), brnyes (brnyes), fut. brnyed (brnyed), to get, obtain, acquire; to meet with, find, B., C., frq.; gáń- (gang)
